story_clusters.headline: Passports, hat-tricks, and statehood demand: Battles shaping Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry elections today
story_clusters.summary: Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry are preparing for Assembly elections, with voters casting ballots across these regions. The elections are notable for issues such as passports, statehood demands, and political "hat-tricks" in Assam and Kerala. Additionally, the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is expected to maintain current interest rates amid concerns over inflation and growth risks.

story_clusters.headline: Raksha Mantri-led IGoM takes stock of India’s readiness in view of evolving West Asia situation
story_clusters.summary: The Aviation Ministry has reduced landing and parking charges for domestic flights by 25% to support airlines amid the West Asia crisis. An inter-ministerial group led by the Raksha Mantri reviewed India’s preparedness in response to the evolving situation in West Asia. Economist Govinda Rao warned that tensions in West Asia could increase fiscal risks.

story_clusters.headline: RBI MPC begins policy meet amid West Asia crisis; decision on Wednesday
story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) began its policy meeting amid a crisis in West Asia, with a decision expected on Wednesday. The government also announced a ₹2.5 lakh crore credit guarantee scheme, administered by the National Credit Guarantee Trustee Company (NCGTC), to support businesses affected by the West Asia crisis. Additionally, American authorities warned of potential cyberattacks from Iran-linked groups targeting critical infrastructure.

Additionally, the RBI proposed that bank boards should prioritize policy oversight over daily operations.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) projects India's economic growth at 6.9% for the current fiscal year, with the repo rate remaining unchanged. The RBI Governor has warned that the West Asia conflict could hinder growth and raise imported inflation, while also noting challenges posed by a weak rupee.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) maintained its current policy stance, avoiding significant changes while preserving flexibility for future adjustments. The RBI indicated that liquidity conditions would be managed to keep overnight rates near the policy rate. As a result, loan EMIs are expected to remain stable, though economic uncertainty persists.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has projected retail inflation at 4.6% for FY27, within the government’s mandated target, while retaining GDP growth at 7.6% for FY26 and setting FY27 growth at 6.9%. It also introduced its first-ever core inflation projection of 4.4% for FY27 amid risks from the West Asia conflict. The RBI remains in a "wait-and-watch" mode, balancing growth and inflation concerns.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has issued multiple draft amendment directions for public consultation, including proposals related to the Trade Receivables Discounting System, Branch Authorisation, Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital computation, and the Investment Fluctuation Reserve. Each set of draft directions invites stakeholder feedback before finalization, covering regulatory updates in areas such as capital adequacy, branch operations, and risk management frameworks. The RBI has not yet finalized these amendments, as they remain in the public consultation phase.

articles.title: Analysis of Notifications and Circulars for Week Ending 5th April 2026
articles.summary: The government issued amendments to tax notifications and circulars for the week ending April 5, 2026, aimed at simplifying compliance, promoting investment, and reducing penalties. These changes reflect a major shift toward a more taxpayer-friendly tax regime. The updates were published by TaxGuru, a platform covering tax-related developments in India.
articles.raw_summary: The latest amendments aim to simplify compliance and promote investment while reducing penalties. The update signals a major shift toward a taxpayer-friendly regime. The post Analysis of Notifications and Circulars for Week Ending 5th April 2026 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Major Changes Effective 1st April 2026 related to Income Tax, GST, Corporate Law
articles.summary: The Indian government will implement wide-ranging reforms in income tax, GST, and corporate law starting April 1, 2026. These changes aim to simplify compliance, enhance transparency, and strengthen financial discipline across labor, tax, and banking systems. The reforms are expected to impact businesses, taxpayers, and financial institutions nationwide.
articles.raw_summary: Wide-ranging reforms across labour, tax, GST, and banking systems come into force from April 2026. These changes aim to simplify compliance while strengthening transparency and financial discipline. The post Major Changes Effective 1st April 2026 related to Income Tax, GST, Corporate Law appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Cabinet clears amendments to women reservation Bill
articles.summary: The Indian Cabinet has approved amendments to the Women Reservation Bill. The bill aims to reserve one-third of seats in the Lok Sabha and state legislatures for women. This move is part of efforts to enhance women's political representation in governance. The next steps involve parliamentary proceedings for the bill's passage.

articles.title: Analysis of Companies Compliance Facilitation Scheme, 2026 (CCFS-2026)
articles.summary: The Companies Compliance Facilitation Scheme, 2026 (CCFS-2026) allows companies to regularize pending compliance filings at reduced costs, offering penalty relief as a final opportunity. The scheme is aimed at easing compliance burdens for businesses. It is a government initiative to facilitate better regulatory adherence. The analysis highlights its key provisions and implications for corporate compliance.
articles.raw_summary: CCFS-2026 allows companies to clear pending filings at reduced costs. The key takeaway is penalty relief and a final chance to regularize compliance. The post Analysis of Companies Compliance Facilitation Scheme, 2026 (CCFS-2026) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Revised GST Input Service Distributor (ISD) mechanism wef 01st April 2026
articles.summary: The Indian government has revised the GST Input Service Distributor (ISD) mechanism, making it mandatory for businesses to distribute Input Tax Credit (ITC) for common services through ISD starting April 1, 2026. This change eliminates the optional use of cross-charge for such transactions. The new rule aims to streamline tax compliance and reduce ambiguity in service tax distribution among businesses.
articles.raw_summary: The law now requires businesses to distribute ITC for common services through ISD. This eliminates optional use of cross-charge for such transactions. The post Revised GST Input Service Distributor (ISD) mechanism wef 01st April 2026 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Women’s Bill Gets Cabinet Nod, Set For 2029 Rollout
articles.summary: The Indian government has approved the Women's Reservation Bill, which will be introduced in Parliament during the upcoming budget session from April 16 to 18. The bill aims to reserve seats for women in legislative bodies and is expected to be passed during this session. The rollout of the bill's provisions is scheduled for 2029.
articles.raw_summary: The Centre has extended the budget session of Parliament and a three-day sitting will be held from April 16 to 18, where the amendment bill is expected to be passed.

articles.title: Comparative Analysis: Old vs New TDS/TCS Framework (1961 vs 2025 Act)
articles.summary: The article compares the existing TDS/TCS provisions under the Income Tax Act, 1961 with the proposed framework in the 2025 Act. It highlights the complexity of current tax deduction/collection rules and suggests that the new framework aims to consolidate sections and introduce coding systems to improve compliance efficiency and reduce ambiguity. The analysis is published by TaxGuru, a platform covering tax-related developments in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue concerns complexity in the existing TDS/TCS provisions. The new framework consolidates sections and introduces coding systems, improving compliance efficiency and reducing ambiguity. The post Comparative Analysis: Old vs New TDS/TCS Framework (1961 vs 2025 Act) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Courts Must 'Nip In The Bud' Proceedings By Unlicensed Money Lenders; Investigation Against Them Need Not Await New Law : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court clarified that existing laws can be used to take action against unlicensed money lenders without waiting for new legislation. The Court emphasized that State money lending laws and the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita already provide the necessary provisions for enforcement. This ruling directs authorities to act promptly against unauthorized lending activities.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court recently clarified that its earlier order closing the suo motu proceedings on unauthorised money lending should not be construed to mean that no law presently exists on the subject or that enforcement actions must wait for fresh legislation by States or Union Territories. The Court clarified that existing provisions under the State money lending laws and the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita be invoked against unlicensed money lenders. articles.title: Section 68 Addition Deleted Due to Explained Cash Sales in Books
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that cash deposits during demonetization, backed by recorded sales in books, cannot be taxed under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act. This decision clarifies that explained cash sales in financial records are not subject to unexplained income provisions. The ruling impacts tax assessments for businesses and individuals with cash transactions during the demonetization period. The post was published on TaxGuru.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved cash deposits during demonetization treated as unexplained. The Tribunal held that deposits backed by recorded sales cannot be taxed under Section 68. The post Section 68 Addition Deleted Due to Explained Cash Sales in Books appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Reassessment u/s. 148 based on mere change of opinion is impermissible in law
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Pune ruled that reassessment notices issued under section 148 of the Income Tax Act based solely on a change of opinion—such as an audit objection—are legally impermissible. The tribunal held that such notices are invalid and must be quashed. This decision impacts tax reassessment procedures and reinforces legal safeguards against arbitrary tax demands.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Pune held that reopening of assessment under section 148 of the Income Tax Act based on audit objection is merely change of opinion and the same is impermissible in law. Accordingly, notice issued u/s. 148 is not valid and is liable to be quashed. The post Reassessment u/s. 148 based on mere change of opinion is impermissible in law appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Genuine Buyer Cannot Be Punished: Landmark GST Judgment by Karnataka HC
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that genuine buyers cannot be penalized for suppliers' failure to deposit GST. The court held that action must be taken against defaulting suppliers rather than bona fide purchasers. This landmark judgment clarifies that Input Tax Credit (ITC) cannot be denied to genuine buyers due to suppliers' non-compliance. The decision reinforces accountability in the GST framework and protects taxpayers from unintended penalties.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether ITC can be denied if suppliers fail to deposit tax. The Court held that bona fide purchasers cannot be penalized, and action must be taken against defaulting suppliers. The post Genuine Buyer Cannot Be Punished: Landmark GST Judgment by Karnataka HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Department Cannot Adjust VAT ITC Post-GST When Law Mandates Cash Refund
articles.summary: The court ruled that the Department cannot adjust VAT Input Tax Credit (ITC) post-GST when the law mandates a cash refund. The judgment emphasized that Section 142(6) of the GST law overrides conflicting departmental circulars, ensuring taxpayers receive refunds in cash rather than adjustments. This decision provides relief to businesses facing refund denials for pre-GST VAT deposits made through ITC.
articles.raw_summary: The case addressed denial of refund of VAT pre-deposit made through ITC. The Court held that Section 142(6) mandates cash refund and overrides conflicting departmental circulars, ensuring taxpayer relief. articles.title: West Bengal Assembly elections: Promise of jobs remains big highlight of poll manifestos of Left Front and Congress
articles.summary: The Left Front and Congress have released their election manifestos for the West Bengal Assembly elections, with job creation and industrialization as key themes. The Left Front promises heavy and medium-scale industrial projects, including new software and hardware parks, while the Congress manifesto appears to align closely with the ruling Trinamool Congress's promises. These proposals could shape future economic policies if the parties come to power.
articles.raw_summary: Left Front manifesto promises initiatives for heavy and medium scale industrialisation, setting up of new software and hardware parks; Congress manifesto looks like a copy of the Trinamool Congress poll promises

articles.title: Supreme Court refuses to stay Adani’s JAL resolution plan in setback for Vedanta
articles.summary: The Supreme Court allowed Adani Enterprises' resolution plan for Jaipur Udaipur Expressways Limited (JAL) to proceed but mandated that major decisions require approval from the National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT). The court also agreed to hear Vedanta's challenge against this plan on April 10. This ruling impacts corporate governance and regulatory oversight in India's infrastructure sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court allowed Adani Enterprises’ JAL resolution plan to proceed but said major decisions need NCLAT approval, while agreeing to hear Vedanta’s challenge on April 10.

articles.title: As govt challenges ‘constitutional morality’ in Sabarimala reference, a look at its evolution in courts
articles.summary: The Centre has argued in court that 'constitutional morality' is a vague concept that oversteps written statutes, challenging its use in the Sabarimala case. The article traces the legal evolution of this concept in Indian courts, highlighting its role in shaping rights and governance. The dispute involves the government, judiciary, and civil society groups advocating for gender justice. The outcome could influence future interpretations of constitutional principles and women's rights.
articles.raw_summary: ‘Vague, indeterminate concept’: Centre argues it oversteps the written statute but the legal background does showcase organic development.

articles.title: Maharashtra: National Green Tribunal grants interim stay on tree cutting in Nashik for Kumbh
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal has granted an interim stay on tree cutting in Nashik, Maharashtra, following a petition by a local environmental activist challenging the Nashik Municipal Corporation's tree-felling activities for the Kumbh event. The tribunal's order halts further tree cutting until the matter is resolved. This decision highlights environmental concerns and legal oversight in urban development projects.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal was hearing a petition by a city-based environmental activist who challenged the Nashik Municipal Corporation’s ongoing tree-cutting.

articles.title: GST Registration Restoration Allowed Due to Compliance with Court-Imposed Conditions
articles.summary: The article reports that a court has allowed the restoration of GST registration for a taxpayer whose registration was canceled due to non-filing of returns. The court ruled that the registration can be revived if the taxpayer clears pending returns and dues as per prescribed conditions. This decision impacts tax compliance and administrative processes under India's Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved cancellation of GST registration for non-filing of returns. articles.title: No TCS on Compounding Fees as Illegal Mining Lacks Legal Contract or Licence: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that compounding fees from illegal mining activities are not subject to Tax Collected at Source (TCS) under Section 206C(1C) because such mining lacks a legal contract or license. The court clarified that the tax provision applies only to lawful mining arrangements involving a lease or license. This decision impacts tax compliance and enforcement in the mining sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court upheld that Section 206C(1C) applies only to lawful mining arrangements involving lease or licence. It ruled that compounding fees from illegal mining are not subject to TCS. The post No TCS on Compounding Fees as Illegal Mining Lacks Legal Contract or Licence: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Sabarimala Reference | Never Understood What Transformative Constitutionalism Is : Solicitor General Questions 'Constitutional Morality'
articles.summary: The Solicitor General (SG) argued in the Sabarimala reference case that 'constitutional morality' cannot be used as a legal standard under Article 25 of the Constitution, which guarantees religious freedom. The SG's stance challenges the interpretation of constitutional principles in the context of religious practices. This debate is part of ongoing legal discussions about the balance between constitutional morality and religious rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: SG argued that constitutional morality cannot be used as a yardstick under Article 25 of the Constitution.

articles.title: No CAAR Decision on E-Scooter Parts Classification After Application Withdrawal
articles.summary: The Customs Authority for Advance Rulings (CAAR) allowed the withdrawal of an application seeking classification of e-scooter parts for customs duty purposes before a decision was issued. The withdrawal was permitted under Regulation 20, leaving the classification and duty issues unresolved. This case highlights the procedural aspects of advance rulings in customs matters and their impact on trade and tax policy.
articles.raw_summary: The case addresses withdrawal of an advance ruling request before its pronouncement. The authority permitted withdrawal under Regulation 20, leaving the classification and duty issues undecided. The post No CAAR Decision on E-Scooter Parts Classification After Application Withdrawal appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: The Notion of the Chinese Nation: How Beijing’s New Ethnic Law Pushes Taiwan Further Away
articles.summary: The article examines how China's new ethnic law, which emphasizes a unified 'Chinese nation' identity, contrasts with Taiwan's approach to language and identity. This divergence deepens the political and societal divide across the Taiwan Strait. The analysis highlights how differing conceptualizations of nationhood contribute to tensions between Beijing and Taipei, with potential implications for regional stability and international relations.
articles.raw_summary: Contrasting approaches to language and identity highlight a widening divide in how each side conceptualizes nationhood – part of the political and societal gap across the Taiwan Strait.

articles.title: Maximum Penalty Imposed Due to 2804-Day Delay in Filing MR-1 Form
articles.summary: A company was penalized for failing to file the mandatory Form MR-1 for appointing a Whole-Time Director, resulting in a 2,804-day delay. The authority ruled that prolonged non-compliance constituted a continuing default and imposed the maximum penalties under Section 450 of the Companies Act, 2013. The case highlights strict enforcement of corporate governance regulations in India.
articles.raw_summary: The case involved failure to file mandatory Form MR-1 for appointment of a Whole-Time Director. The authority held that prolonged non-compliance constituted a continuing default and imposed maximum penalties under Section 450. articles.title: Section 68 Addition Deleted Due to Double Taxation of Recorded Sales Receipts
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that taxing SBN deposits as unexplained income, despite being part of recorded sales, amounts to impermissible double taxation. The tribunal deleted the Section 68 addition, clarifying that taxing the same amount twice is not allowed under the law. This decision impacts how businesses report and are taxed on their sales receipts.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved SBN deposits treated as unexplained despite being part of recorded sales. The Tribunal held that taxing the same amount again results in impermissible double taxation. articles.title: SC Upholds Life Term of Man Who Killed His Wife After Being Drunk
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the life imprisonment of a man convicted of killing his wife while intoxicated. The ruling reaffirmed that a medically certified and consistent dying declaration can alone sustain a conviction. The decision underscores the judiciary's role in ensuring justice in cases of gender-based violence. The case examined whether such a declaration was sufficient evidence for conviction.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether a dying declaration alone could sustain conviction. articles.title: Authorities Cannot Direct Removal Of "National" From Trust Name Under Maharashtra Public Trusts Act: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that authorities under the Maharashtra Public Trusts Act do not have jurisdiction to direct a change in the name of a trust. The Court observed that the statutory scheme of the Act does not contemplate any enquiry into the suitability of the name of a trust, and therefore, no such power can be exercised by the authorities. Justice Sharmila U. Deshmukh was hearing a writ petition filed by the National Egg Co-Ordination Committee challenging the order dated 14.09.202
articles.raw_summary: The Bombay High Court has held that authorities under the Maharashtra Public Trusts Act do not have jurisdiction to direct a change in the name of a trust. The Court observed that the statutory scheme of the Act does not contemplate any enquiry into the suitability of the name of a trust, and therefore, no such power can be exercised by the authorities. Justice Sharmila U. Deshmukh was hearing a writ petition filed by the National Egg Co-Ordination Committee challenging the order dated 14.09.202

The decision highlights flaws in relying solely on disclosure statements for evidence in criminal trials.

articles.title: Civil Court Can Decide Appointment of Sajjadanashin in Waqf Institutions: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that civil courts in India can decide on the appointment of Sajjadanashin (spiritual heads) in Waqf institutions. The court distinguished the roles of Sajjadanashin (religious head) and Mutawalli (administrator), noting that while the former is a religious matter, the latter pertains to administration. This ruling clarifies the legal authority of civil courts in such appointments.
articles.raw_summary: The office of the mutawalli and Sajjadanashin cannot be said to be one and the same  Sajjadanashin is the spiritual head of Waqf and declaration of Sajjadanashin is a religious affair, however, role of Mutawalli of a Waqf only pertains to the administration and management of the Waqf. This decision clarifies the legal interpretation of tax exemptions for trusts in India.

articles.title: Markets in Pieces
articles.summary: The article by Disha Joshi and Soham Gupta explores how arbitration and public policy intersect in competition law, emphasizing market coherence over individual correctness. It argues that arbitration shapes incentives by validating contracts that impact competitive conditions, even though it does not directly regulate markets. The discussion is framed within India's legal and governance systems, particularly concerning how contractual arrangements influence market behavior.
articles.raw_summary: **Disha Joshi and Soham Gupta Debates on arbitration and public policy typically focus on illegality, morality, or judicial review. This article advances a different claim. In competition law, public policy concerns market coherence rather than individual correctness. While arbitration does not regulate markets, it shapes incentives by validating contractual arrangements that affect competitive conditions. articles.summary: JPMorgan has modeled three potential market outcomes based on a US-Iran war scenario. A bullish outcome would involve de-escalation in West Asia or a US Federal Reserve policy shift, leading to a broad market rally, lower bond yields, falling oil prices, tighter credit spreads, and a weaker US dollar. The scenarios are speculative and depend on geopolitical developments and central bank actions.
articles.raw_summary: A bullish scenario will be when there is proper de-escalation in West Asia or there is a policy pivot by the US Federal Reserve. In such a scenario, they see a broad "everything rally" where equities advance, bond yields drop, oil prices fall, credit spreads tighten and the US Dollar weakens.

This move is part of broader discussions on legal uniformity in India. The state's action reflects a policy decision with potential nationwide implications.

articles.title: Cash toll payments end April 10; UPI allowed at 1.25x fee without FASTag
articles.summary: From April 10, cash payments at highway toll plazas in India will be discontinued, with FASTag becoming the primary payment method. Vehicles without a valid FASTag can pay via UPI at 1.25 times the toll fee, as per government regulations. This move aims to streamline toll collection and promote digital payments. The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) is overseeing the transition.

articles.title: Bengal assembly elections: After SIR, number of voters in Mamata Banerjee's seat down 25%

The revisions occurred under the Systematic Identification of Errors (SIR) process, which has drawn attention to the fairness of electoral procedures.

articles.title: Homebuyers Cannot Claim ITC Benefit in Fully Post-GST Projects: GSTAT
articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) ruled that homebuyers cannot claim Input Tax Credit (ITC) benefits in projects fully governed under the GST regime. The tribunal held that since construction, agreements, and payments occurred entirely post-GST, prices already included ITC, and no additional benefit needed to be passed on. This decision clarifies the tax treatment of real estate projects under the GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: GSTAT held that no anti-profiteering violation arises where construction, agreement, and payments occur entirely in the GST regime. Since prices already factored ITC, no benefit was required to be passed on. The post Homebuyers Cannot Claim ITC Benefit in Fully Post-GST Projects: GSTAT appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz won’t resolve global oil crisis
articles.summary: Geopolitical conflicts have damaged oil refineries and pipelines in multiple countries, reducing global oil supply. High insurance rates and shipping costs further strain the market. The Strait of Hormuz's reopening and a ceasefire may not resolve the crisis. India, as a major oil importer, could face economic and policy challenges due to these disruptions.
articles.raw_summary: Damage to oil refineries and pipelines in many countries will limit supply, while insurance rates and shipping costs may remain prohibitively high.

articles.summary: The Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) corrected a calculation error in an anti-profiteering order. The tribunal clarified the correct per square foot benefit including GST and directed proper computation. This ruling impacts businesses subject to anti-profiteering laws under GST. The decision ensures accurate financial adjustments for consumers.

articles.title: MeitY to extend deadline for IT Rules on news content takedown in social media
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is extending the deadline for IT Rules concerning the takedown of news content on social media platforms. MeitY clarified that the proposal empowers the Ministry of Information & Broadcasting (MIB) to decide on content related to news and current affairs. This move aims to strengthen regulatory oversight over digital news dissemination and social media platforms in India.
articles.raw_summary: Clarifying the intent behind the proposal, MeitY said, 'We have only introduced a clarification to give power to MIB (Ministry of Information & Broadcasting) to decide on content related to news, current affairs.'

articles.title: Charity ≠ Commerce – ITAT Grants U/s 12AB Registration to Trust Despite Sales Activity
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a trust's activities, despite including sales, were charitable in nature and directed the grant of registration under Section 12AB of the Income Tax Act. The trust had previously been denied registration on the grounds that its activities were commercial. This decision clarifies the distinction between charity and commerce for tax-exempt trusts in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was rejection of trust registration treating activities as commercial. The Tribunal held activities were charitable and directed grant of registration under Section 12AB. The post Charity ≠ Commerce   ITAT Grants U/s 12AB Registration to Trust Despite Sales Activity appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Urgent cases to be mentioned only before CJI-led bench: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (SC) has ruled that urgent cases must now be mentioned only before a bench led by the Chief Justice of India (CJI), even if the CJI is unavailable or presiding over a constitution bench. This changes the usual practice where urgent matters were handled by the seniormost available judge. The decision impacts how cases are prioritized and listed for hearing at the apex court.
articles.raw_summary: According to the usual practice, urgent matters are mentioned for listing and hearing before the seniormost judge of the apex court if the CJI is either unavailable or busy heading a constitution bench.

articles.title: Strait of Hormuz hopes crushed hours after ceasefire as Iran closes waterway again; ‘alternate’ routes announced
articles.summary: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for global oil shipments, citing risks of sea mines. Hours after a ceasefire announcement, the closure disrupted shipping routes, prompting Iran to propose alternative paths. The Strait is vital for India’s oil imports from the Middle East, raising concerns about energy supply disruptions and potential policy measures to mitigate risks.
articles.raw_summary: Iran announced alternative shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz after its closure, citing risks of sea mines in the main channel of the key waterway.

articles.title: Government raises gas supply to fertiliser plants, boosts fuel allocation amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) has raised gas supply to fertilizer plants by 5%, reaching around 95% of their six-month average consumption starting April 9, 2026. This decision is based on available inventory and scheduled LNG cargo arrivals amid tensions in West Asia. The move aims to ensure adequate fuel supply for fertilizer production, which is critical for India's agricultural sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) has increased gas allocation to fertilizer plants by 5% to around 95% of their six-month average consumption, effective April 9, 2026, based on available inventory and scheduled LNG cargo arrivals.

articles.title: 100% tariff threat looms over patented drugs; generics exempt for now
articles.summary: The government has proposed a 100% tariff on patented pharmaceutical products, with implementation timelines of 120 days for large companies and 180 days for smaller firms. Generics are exempt from this tariff. The move aims to address pricing concerns in the pharmaceutical sector but could impact access to patented drugs in India.
articles.raw_summary: A 100% tariff on patented pharmaceutical products has been proposed, with implementation timelines of 120 days for large companies and 180 days for smaller firms.

The flagship event at Bhuinpur in Khordha district included 34 projects under Begunia block, while two additional projects were initiated in Ramdaspur, Cuttack. This initiative reflects the state's policy-driven push for industrial growth and employment generation.
articles.raw_summary: The flagship event at Bhuinpur in Khordha district saw the rollout of 34 projects under Begunia block, while two additional projects were taken up at Ramdaspur in Cuttack.

articles.title: Crude prices climb back towards $97 as Strait of Hormuz remains under pressure
articles.summary: Oil prices rose after a recent drop, driven by ongoing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route. The U.S. Vice President Vance suggested the strait was beginning to reopen, but concerns over renewed hostilities kept prices elevated. Conflicting reports on tanker movements added to the uncertainty. The situation could impact global oil supply and prices, with potential implications for India's energy security.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices edged higher Thursday after a significant daily drop, as Middle East tensions and uncertainty surrounding the Strait of Hormuz kept markets on edge. Despite conflicting reports on tanker movement, US Vice President Vance indicated the strait was beginning to reopen. Prices remain elevated due to persistent concerns over potential renewed hostilities.

The situation could have implications for global oil supply and energy markets.

articles.title: Nvidia acquisition of SchedMD sparks worry among AI specialists about software access
articles.summary: Nvidia announced its acquisition of SchedMD, the company behind Slurm, an open-source software used to schedule computing tasks. Slurm is essential for training large language models like those powering AI chatbots and is also used on government supercomputers for tasks such as weather forecasting and nuclear research. The deal has raised concerns among AI specialists about potential restrictions on software access, which could impact AI development and critical infrastructure.

Slurm also runs on government supercomputers which help forecast the weather and develop nuclear weapons.

Oceans absorb most of the planet's excess heat, but the consequences of rising SST—such as marine ecosystem disruptions and extreme weather—are often overshadowed by discussions on air temperatures. This trend underscores the urgent need for climate action, including policy measures to address ocean warming and its impacts.

articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to launch strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges if Iran continues to block the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil shipping route. The warning escalates tensions between the U.S. and Iran, raising concerns about potential disruptions to global oil supplies. The Strait of Hormuz is a key chokepoint for oil exports, including those to India, making this development significant for energy security.
articles.raw_summary: Trump warns of major strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges Tuesday, demanding Iran open the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions over the vital oil route

articles.title: Arunachal Pradesh: Yak reintroduction drive in Mechuka to boost tribal livelihoods
articles.summary: The Arunachal Pradesh government distributed six yaks—four females and two males—to local farmers in the Shi-Yomi district as part of a program to revive yak-based income for tribal communities. The initiative aims to boost livelihoods in Mechuka by reintroducing yaks, which are culturally and economically significant to the region. The program is being implemented in collaboration with local authorities to support tribal farmers.
articles.raw_summary: As part of the programme, six yak—including four females and two males—were distributed to local farmers to help revive yak-based income in the Shi-Yomi district.

articles.title: India raises gas supply for fertilizer firms as imports improve
articles.summary: India has boosted natural gas allocation to urea plants to 90% of average consumption, up from 70-75%, as supply improves following Middle East crisis disruptions. This decision, based on inventory and scheduled LNG arrivals, aims to support the upcoming monsoon planting season.

The vessel is equipped with a two-stroke propulsion system, air lubrication technology, and shaft generators to improve fuel efficiency and reduce emissions. This deal is part of efforts to enhance India's LNG shipping capabilities and aligns with broader energy transition goals.

This move supports India's National Quantum Mission and prepares businesses for future technological advancements.

articles.title: Strategists say oil shock may push interest rates higher as bond markets adjust

They allege that presiding officers conducted 'mini trials' instead of a prima-facie assessment, calling it a threat to constitutional mechanisms. The opposition plans to pursue legal avenues, arguing that delaying accountability undermines democracy.

articles.title: HC frees man who raped 3-yr-old, says gravity of offence can’t be sole ground for denying early release
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court granted early release to a man who had spent over 22 years in prison for raping a 3-year-old child. The court ruled that the severity of the offense alone cannot justify denying early release, overturning the Sentence Review Board's 10 rejections of his plea. The decision highlights the balance between punishment and rehabilitation in India's criminal justice system.
articles.raw_summary: The man had spent over 22 years in prison. His plea for early release had been rejected 10 times by the Sentence Review Board.

The crisis stems from geopolitical tensions affecting fuel supply chains, with potential implications for India's energy imports and aviation sector.

The development could impact India's energy costs and economic policies, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.

articles.summary: Murshidabad, a district in West Bengal where 70% of residents are Muslim, has become a politically sensitive area due to the Babri mosque dispute. By March 29, the cell had acted on 17 cases involving 27 profiles across platforms, registered one FIR, and arrested the prime accused. The cell claims a 100% success rate in removing problematic content. This initiative raises questions about digital surveillance, free speech, and law enforcement's role in regulating online content during elections.

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: Why Anthropic’s new AI model Mythos is a moment of reckoning
articles.summary: Anthropic, an AI company, has developed a new AI model called Mythos that can identify thousands of critical security flaws, some decades old. Due to risks of misuse, the model is not being released publicly but is being tested by about 40 companies, including major tech firms, to fix vulnerabilities before attackers exploit them. This development marks a significant shift in cybersecurity practices and raises concerns about AI's role in both securing and threatening digital infrastructure.
articles.raw_summary: Anthropic's new AI model, Mythos, can find thousands of critical security flaws, some decades old. Due to potential misuse, it is not being released publicly. Instead, about 40 companies, including tech giants, are testing it to fix bugs before attackers can exploit them. This development signals a major shift in cybersecurity.
